content.problemStatement:
Write a program which does the following:


Find the area of a square of side 14 cm.
Also, find the cost of painting the square if the paint costs is $7 per cm2cm^2cm2.
Initialise the following  `variables`  in your program and use them in the computation

**`s`**  to store the length of side the square.
**`area`**  to store the area of the square.
**`cost`**  to store the cost of painting.


Output  **`area`**  and  **`cost`**  to the console on separate lines.
content.outputFormat: First line: area of the square (in cm²). Second line: cost of painting the square (in dollars).

solution.approach: The side length of the square is given as 14 cm. Compute area = s * s. Compute cost = area * 7. Print both values on separate lines. The computation is O(1) time and O(1) memory.

code.C++: #include <bits/stdc++.h>\nusing namespace std;\nint main(){int T=1;for(int t=0;t<T;++t){int s=14;int area=s*s;int cost=area*7;cout<<area<<'\\n'<<cost<<'\\n';}return 0;}
code.Java: public class Main{public static void main(String[] args){int T=1;for(int t=0;t<T;++t){int s=14;int area=s*s;int cost=area*7;System.out.println(area);System.out.println(cost);}}}
